name: Request and Document a Move-Out Walkthrough
description: Call the landlord or property manager to schedule a joint move-out walkthrough (also called a pre-move-out or final inspection) — and lock in the conditions, attendees, and documentation that protect the tenant's security deposit.
disclaimer: Not legal advice. Habitability + early-termination skills are scripted to PRESERVE legal posture (request things in writing, refuse verbal-only assurances) — but the operator must consult a tenant attorney for actual rights determination.

context.when_to_use: User has given notice of move-out (or is about to). Ideally schedule the walkthrough 5–10 days BEFORE the actual move-out so the tenant has time to cure any issues the landlord flags. Some states (e.g. California) give the tenant a STATUTORY RIGHT to a pre-move-out inspection — ask the operator to check.
preconditions: User has given written notice of move-out OR has a confirmed lease end date.
User has move-in photos / video (or the original move-in inspection sheet).
User has 2–3 windows of availability for the walkthrough.
User has reviewed the lease for any specific move-out clauses (carpet cleaning, hole patching, paint touch-up requirements).

principles: A pre-move-out walkthrough is a tenant's strongest deposit-protection tool. Schedule it.
Have the walkthrough BEFORE you move your stuff out — the empty unit reveals more, but doing it 5–10 days early gives time to cure flagged issues.
Bring a written checklist and a camera. Photograph every flagged item with the landlord present, timestamped.
If the landlord won't do a joint walkthrough, do a SOLO walkthrough with timestamped photos / video of every wall, appliance, floor, fixture, and outlet — that record is admissible.
Get the landlord to sign or initial a walkthrough checklist if possible. If not, email a recap immediately.
Never accept verbal 'oh that's fine' assurances — those are the items that come back as deductions. Insist on written acknowledgment.

phrases.opener: Hi, my move-out is [date] and I'd like to schedule a pre-move-out walkthrough with you. In my jurisdiction tenants are generally entitled to one — and even where it's not required, it protects both of us. What times work in the next [5–10 days]?
phrases.confirm_purpose: Just so we're aligned: the goal is to walk the unit together, identify anything you'd flag as damage so I can cure it before move-out, and agree on the move-in baseline. Sound good?
phrases.ask_who_attends: Will it be you or another team member doing the walkthrough? Could I get their name?
phrases.ask_for_checklist: Do you have a standard walkthrough form, or should I bring my own checklist?
phrases.ask_for_post_walkthrough_summary: After the walkthrough can you email me a written list of anything you flagged, and what I'd need to do to cure it? I want to make sure we have the same record before move-out.
phrases.deflect_no_walkthrough: No worries if it's not standard for you. I'll do a solo walkthrough with photos then — and I'll email the photos to you the same day so we have a shared record.
phrases.graceful_close: Great — putting [day/time] in my calendar. I'll bring my move-in photos and a checklist. See you then.

tactics.name: Open by naming the right and the timing
tactics.script: Use the `opener`. Naming the legal frame ('tenants are generally entitled') signals the tenant knows the process without being adversarial.

tactics.name: State the purpose explicitly
tactics.script: Use `confirm_purpose`. Pre-naming the goal — cure flagged items, agree on baseline — prevents the walkthrough from devolving into a vague conversation.

tactics.name: Identify the attendee and ask for their checklist
tactics.script: Use `ask_who_attends` and `ask_for_checklist`. Knowing the form in advance lets the tenant address items the landlord cares about.

tactics.name: Pre-arm the written summary
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_post_walkthrough_summary`. The post-walkthrough email is the document that wins (or prevents) the deposit dispute.

tactics.name: Fall back to solo walkthrough
tactics.when: Landlord refuses to do a joint walkthrough.
tactics.script: Use `deflect_no_walkthrough`. Then do a thorough solo walkthrough with timestamped photos and video and email a full set to the landlord same day with a written summary. The refusal itself helps the tenant's posture if disputed later.

boundaries: Do NOT skip the walkthrough because the landlord seems friendly — friendly landlords change their mind when they see a contractor quote.
Do NOT sign any move-out condition document on the spot unless you've reviewed it. Take it home and review.
Do NOT agree to verbal-only assurances about what's 'fine.' Get every flagged item in writing.
Do NOT raise unrelated complaints (rent disputes, prior issues) during the walkthrough — it muddies the record.
Do NOT pay any move-out fees on the walkthrough call — those belong in the final accounting.
success_signals: Joint walkthrough scheduled BEFORE move-out date.
Landlord agrees to send a written list of flagged items afterwards.
Landlord names the attendee and is willing to use a checklist.
Pre-move-out gives the tenant time to cure any flagged item before final.
failure_signals: Landlord schedules walkthrough only on move-out day (no time to cure).
Landlord refuses to send written summary.
Landlord insists on signed releases or waivers at the walkthrough.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm date, time, attendee name, and that a written summary will follow. Operator should email a recap within 1 hour confirming the appointment.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Plan and execute a solo walkthrough with full photo / video documentation. Email the landlord a same-day recap with photos attached, noting the request for a joint walkthrough was declined.
follow_ups: Bring move-in photos / video to the walkthrough on phone or printed.
Bring a written checklist covering every room, appliance, wall, floor, fixture.
Take a new photo of every item the landlord flags WITH the landlord visible if possible.
Send a recap email within 24 hours summarising what was flagged and the cure plan.
Calendar a follow-up cure-by date if anything was flagged.

required_user_info: Lease copy with any move-out clauses highlighted
Two or three pre-move-out walkthrough windows
